com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXProject

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXObject, IXWriteableObject

    public interface IXProject
extends IXWriteableObject
    Interface representing a project.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Deprecated Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXStudy> getAllStudies()
      Get the Studies for this project
      java.util.Set<IXBuildList> getBuildLists()
      Get list of buildlists contained in this project.
      java.util.Set<IXConfiguration> getConfigurations()
      Get the configurations for this project
      java.util.Set<IXDesign> getDesigns()
      Deprecated. 
      Avoid using this method as this can load significant data into memory when there are many designs in a project thus causing performance and capacity issues. To load specific designs, use getDesigns(Class, List)
      <T extends IXDesign>
      java.util.Map<java.lang.String,? extends T>      		 getDesigns(java.lang.Class<T> designClass, java.util.List<java.lang.String> ids)
      Get a collection of designs with the specified Unique IDentifiers (UIDs).
      java.util.Iterator<? extends IXEngineeringChangeOrder> getEngineeringChangeOrders(java.lang.String category, java.lang.String releaseLevel)
      Get the engineering change orders in the project for the specified parameters
      java.util.Set<IXFolder> getFolders()
      get the folder manager in this project.
      java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode>> getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations()
      Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the project
      java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getFunctionalModuleCodes()
      Get list of functional module codes contained in this project.
      java.util.Iterator<? extends IXFunctionDesign> getFunctionDesigns()
      Get the functional designs contained in this project.
      java.util.Set<IXHarnessDesign> getHarnessDesigns()
      Deprecated. 
      Avoid using this method as this can load significant data into memory when there are many harness designs in a project thus causing performance and capacity issues. To load specific designs, use getDesigns(Class, List)
      java.util.Set<IXIntegratorDesign> getIntegratorDesigns()
      Get list of integrator designs contained in this project.
      java.util.Set<IXLogicDesign> getLogicDesigns()
      Get list of logic designs contained in this project.
      java.util.Set<IXObjectTypeInfo> getObjectTypeInfos()
      Get the object type infos for this project
      java.util.Set<IXOption> getOptions()
      Get list of options contained in this project.
      java.util.Set<IXOptionFolder> getOptionsFolders()
      Get the options folders for this project
      java.util.Iterator<? extends IXPlatformArchitectureDesign> getPlatformArchitectureDesigns()
      Get the platform architecture designs contained in this project.
      java.lang.String getPokeHomePrefix()
      Get the poke prefix used in the project preferences.
      java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> getProductionModuleCodeCombinations()
      Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the project
      java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getProductionModuleCodes()
      Get list of production module codes contained in this project.
      java.util.Collection<? extends IXLibraryScopeCode> getScopeScodes()
      Get the scope codes defined on the project.
      java.util.Set<IXTopologyDesign> getTopologyDesigns()
      Get list of topology designs contained in this project.
      boolean isEffectivitySet()
      checks effectivity is selected in this project.

    • Method Detail

      • getDesigns

        @Deprecated
java.util.Set<IXDesign> getDesigns()
        Deprecated. Avoid using this method as this can load significant data into memory when there are many designs in a project thus causing performance and capacity issues. To load specific designs, use getDesigns(Class, List)
        Get list of all designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXDesign> Set of all designs contained in this project.

      • getAllStudies

        java.util.Set<IXStudy> getAllStudies()
        Get the Studies for this project
        Returns:
        Set<IXStudy> a set of study if any exist or an empty Set otherwise

      • isEffectivitySet

        boolean isEffectivitySet()
        checks effectivity is selected in this project.
        Returns:
        boolean tre if effectivity is present otherwise false in this project

      • getPokeHomePrefix

        java.lang.String getPokeHomePrefix()
        Get the poke prefix used in the project preferences.
        Returns:
        returns the poke prefix used in project preferences.

      • getLogicDesigns

        java.util.Set<IXLogicDesign> getLogicDesigns()
        Get list of logic designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXDesign> Set of logic designs contained in this project.

      • getIntegratorDesigns

        java.util.Set<IXIntegratorDesign> getIntegratorDesigns()
        Get list of integrator designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXIntegratorDesign> Set of intgrator designs contained in this project.

      • getTopologyDesigns

        java.util.Set<IXTopologyDesign> getTopologyDesigns()
        Get list of topology designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXTopologyDesigngt; Set of topology designs contained in this project.

      • getHarnessDesigns

        @Deprecated
java.util.Set<IXHarnessDesign> getHarnessDesigns()
        Deprecated. Avoid using this method as this can load significant data into memory when there are many harness designs in a project thus causing performance and capacity issues. To load specific designs, use getDesigns(Class, List)
        Get list of harness designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXHarnessDesigngt; Set of harness designs contained in this project.

      • getPlatformArchitectureDesigns

        java.util.Iterator<? extends IXPlatformArchitectureDesign> getPlatformArchitectureDesigns()
        Get the platform architecture designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Iterator<IXPlatformArchitectureDesign> An Iterator of the platform architecture designs contained in this project.

      • getFunctionDesigns

        java.util.Iterator<? extends IXFunctionDesign> getFunctionDesigns()
        Get the functional designs contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Iterator<IXFunctionDesign> An Iterator of functional designs contained in this project.

      • getBuildLists

        java.util.Set<IXBuildList> getBuildLists()
        Get list of buildlists contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXBuildList> Set of buildlists contained in this project.

      • getOptions

        java.util.Set<IXOption> getOptions()
        Get list of options contained in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXOptiongt; Set of options contained in this project.

      • getFunctionalModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> getFunctionalModuleCodes()
        Get list of functional module codes contained in this project.

        Please note, you should use the codes in IXModuledDesign.getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes() if you are assigning module codes to objects in a design. This will ensure that the correct codes are used if the project codes have been overridden at the design level.

        Returns:
        Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode> Set of functional module codes contained in this project.

      • getProductionModuleCodes

        java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode> getProductionModuleCodes()
        Get list of production module codes contained in this project.

        Please note, you should use the codes in IXModuledDesign.getAvailableProductionModuleCodes() if you are assigning module codes to objects in a design. This will ensure that the correct codes are used if the project codes have been overridden at the design level.

        Returns:
        Set<IXProductionModuleCodegt; Set of production module codes contained in this project.

      • getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations

        java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode>> getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations()
        Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the project

        Please note, you should use the codes in IXModuledDesign.getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations() if you are using this data within the context of a design. This will ensure the crrect codes are used if the project codes have been overridden at the design level.

        Returns:
        Set<Set<IXFunctionalModuleCode>> a set of combinations where each combination is a set of functional module codes

      • getProductionModuleCodeCombinations

        java.util.Set<java.util.Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> getProductionModuleCodeCombinations()
        Gets a list of all the combinations defined for the project

        Please note, you should use the codes in IXModuledDesign.getProductionModuleCodeCombinations() if you are using this data within the context of a design. This will ensure the crrect codes are used if the project codes have been overridden at the design level.

        Returns:
        Set<Set<IXProductionModuleCode>> a set of combinations where each combination is a set of production module codes

      • getFolders

        java.util.Set<IXFolder> getFolders()
        get the folder manager in this project.
        Returns:
        Set<IXFolder> a Set of folders if any exist, or an empty Set otherwise

      • getOptionsFolders

        java.util.Set<IXOptionFolder> getOptionsFolders()
        Get the options folders for this project
        Returns:
        Set<IXOptionFolder> a Set of options folders if any exist, or an empty Set otherwise

      • getConfigurations

        java.util.Set<IXConfiguration> getConfigurations()
        Get the configurations for this project
        Returns:
        Set<IXConfigurationgt; a Set of configurations if any exist, or an empty Set otherwise

      • getObjectTypeInfos

        java.util.Set<IXObjectTypeInfo> getObjectTypeInfos()
        Get the object type infos for this project
        Returns:
        Set<IXObjectTypeInfogt; a Set of object type infos if any exist, or an empty Set otherwise

      • getScopeScodes

        java.util.Collection<? extends IXLibraryScopeCode> getScopeScodes()
        Get the scope codes defined on the project.
        Returns:
        Collection<? extends IXLibraryScopeCode> defined on the Project

      • getEngineeringChangeOrders

        java.util.Iterator<? extends IXEngineeringChangeOrder> getEngineeringChangeOrders(java.lang.String category,
                                                                                  java.lang.String releaseLevel)
        Get the engineering change orders in the project for the specified parameters
        Parameters:
        category - option category name. If the category name is specified, then all the engineering Change Orders under this category will be returned. If null is passed for this parameter, then category will not be considered for filtering the Engineering change orders
        releaseLevel - release level name - If the release level name is specified, then all the Engineering Change Orders having the specified release level will be filtered and retuned. If null is specified, then release level will not be considered for filtering the Engineering change orders

        CAUTION: If both the parameters are not provided it will be an unconstrained query and will take more time and memory based on the number of change orders in the system.

        Returns:
        Iterator of all matching Engineering change orders

      • getDesigns

        <T extends IXDesign> java.util.Map<java.lang.String,? extends T> getDesigns(java.lang.Class<T> designClass,
                                                                            java.util.List<java.lang.String> ids)
        Get a collection of designs with the specified Unique IDentifiers (UIDs). The designs are of the same type, defined by the class parameter. The UIDs passed in must be the UIDs of a design of type IXDesign
        Parameters:
        designClass - The Class that defines the type of the designs
        ids - A list of the UIDs of the designs to get
        Returns:
        A map of the designs that can be accessed, indexed by the id If the design is not available, does not match the specified type or the access is prohibited, there will not be an entry in the result.
